page: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 32 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, the system features 32 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and a shared CPU allocation model. The hardware includes two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance. The AMD EPYC processor achieves a PassMark CPU score of 87,135.2 and a Geekbench single-core score of 3,095.0. Memory performance is strong, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 327.05 GB/sec. The dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs enable high-throughput LLM inference, processing 5,508.2 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M model, and maintaining a strong 220.88 tokens per second for medium 7B models, though performance drops to an average tier of 21.91 tokens per second on large 70B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high-density resource profile by pairing a massive 384.0 GB memory footprint with dual enterprise-grade GPUs, making it highly capable for parallelized workloads without requiring dedicated CPU allocations. This configuration is highly suited for machine learning model training, LLM inference of small-to-medium scale, high-throughput database operations, and intensive multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: The UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server designed for intensive computational and machine learning workloads. It features 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory. The system utilizes a KVM hypervisor and features a shared CPU allocation model with 32 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. Acceleration is driven by two NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace family, providing a total of 96 GB of VRAM. While local storage is not included, the server delivers top-tier CPU performance, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score of 87,135.2 and exceptional multi-threaded decompression speeds. Memory bandwidth is strong, supporting high-throughput operations. This configuration is highly suited for LLM inference, database operations, and parallelized data science workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning and database workloads.
meta_description: The UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-2xL40S server features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs for demanding AI and database workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU server combining dual Ada Lovelace accelerators with massive memory for demanding machine learning and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM for accelerated computing.
32 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs delivering top-tier single and multi-core performance.
384 GB of system memory supporting large-scale data sets and memory-intensive tasks.
Top-tier LLM inference performance on small and medium-sized models.
Excellent fit for machine learning, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
